prompt: NFPA 13 sprinkler systems are primarily designed for:

options.text: Automatic control and suppression of fire in buildings based on occupancy and hazard classification.
Detection of carbon monoxide only.
Kitchen grease exhaust exclusively without water.
Structural steel fireproofing application.

explanation: Correct: Automatic control and suppression of fire in buildings based on occupancy and hazard classification. This aligns with Fire Suppression for this readiness check.

distractorExplanations.b: Incorrect. This does not reflect best practice for Fire Suppression.
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect. This does not reflect best practice for Fire Suppression.

prompt: Means of egress design under life safety codes requires:

options.text: Adequate exit capacity, travel distance limits, and protected exit paths.
Single exit for unlimited occupant load.
No emergency lighting in any occupancy.
Locking all exits during business hours without exception.

explanation: Correct: Adequate exit capacity, travel distance limits, and protected exit paths. This aligns with Safety in the Built Environment for this readiness check.

prompt: Spot-type smoke detectors are typically NOT suitable for:

options.text: High-ceiling atriums where smoke stratifies before reaching the detector.
Small office rooms with flat ceilings.
Corridors with smooth ceilings.
Sleeping rooms with standard ceiling heights.

explanation: Correct: High-ceiling atriums where smoke stratifies before reaching the detector. This aligns with Detection and Alarm for this readiness check.

distractorExplanations.b: Incorrect. This does not reflect best practice for Detection and Alarm.

distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect. This does not reflect best practice for Detection and Alarm.

prompt: Hot work permits in fire prevention programs require:

options.text: Authorization, fire watch, and control of combustibles in the work area.
Welding without any fire extinguisher present.
No communication with the AHJ.
Permanent exemption for all maintenance staff.

explanation: Correct: Authorization, fire watch, and control of combustibles in the work area. This aligns with Fire Prevention: Programs, Materials, Processes, and Environments for this readiness check.

distractorExplanations.b: Incorrect. This does not reflect best practice for Fire Prevention: Programs, Materials, Processes, and Environments.

prompt: Hot work permits in fire prevention programs require:

options.text: Welding without any fire extinguisher present.
Authorization, fire watch, and control of combustibles in the work area.

Permanent exemption for all maintenance staff.

prompt: Process hazard analysis in industrial facilities often uses:

options.text: HAZOP or what-if checklists to identify deviation scenarios and safeguards.
No documentation of safeguards.
Elimination of all automatic shutdown systems.
Only qualitative color codes without scenarios.
